E8-1.name: Application Control
E8-1.description: Prevent execution of unapproved/malicious programs including .exe, DLL, scripts, installers

E8-2.name: Patch Applications
E8-2.description: Patch/mitigate vulnerabilities in internet-facing and high-risk applications within 48hrs for critical, 2 weeks otherwise

E8-3.name: Configure Microsoft Office Macro Settings
E8-3.description: Block macros from the internet, only allow vetted macros in trusted locations

E8-4.name: User Application Hardening
E8-4.description: Configure web browsers and PDF viewers to block ads, Java, Flash, and other risky content

E8-5.name: Restrict Administrative Privileges
E8-5.description: Limit admin privileges to those who need them, revalidate regularly, no internet/email/web from privileged accounts

E8-6.name: Patch Operating Systems
E8-6.description: Patch/mitigate OS vulnerabilities within 48hrs for critical, 2 weeks otherwise; replace unsupported OS

E8-7.name: Multi-factor Authentication
E8-7.description: MFA for VPNs, RDP, SSH, internet-facing services and privileged access

E8-8.name: Regular Backups
E8-8.description: Regular backups of important data, software and config settings, stored disconnected, tested regularly

GV.RM.name: Risk Management Strategy
GV.RM.description: Priorities, constraints, risk tolerance, and risk appetite are established and communicated

GV.SC.name: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
GV.SC.description: Cyber supply chain risk management processes are identified and managed

ID.AM.name: Asset Management
ID.AM.description: Assets that enable the organisation to achieve business purposes are identified and managed

ID.RA.name: Risk Assessment
ID.RA.description: The organisation understands the cybersecurity risk to operations, assets, and individuals

PR.AA.name: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
PR.AA.description: Access is limited to authorized users, services, and hardware; managed commensurate with risk

PR.AT.name: Awareness and Training
PR.AT.description: The organisation's personnel and partners are provided cybersecurity awareness education

PR.DS.name: Data Security
PR.DS.description: Data is managed consistent with the organisation's risk strategy to protect CIA

PR.IR.name: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
PR.IR.description: Security architectures are managed to protect asset CIA and organisational resilience

PR.PS.name: Platform Security
PR.PS.description: Hardware, software, and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with risk

DE.CM.name: Continuous Monitoring
DE.CM.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, IOCs, and other potentially adverse events

DE.AE.name: Adverse Event Analysis
DE.AE.description: Anomalies, IOCs, and other potentially adverse events are analysed to characterise the events

RS.MA.name: Incident Management
RS.MA.description: Responses to detected incidents are managed

RS.AN.name: Incident Analysis
RS.AN.description: Investigations are conducted to ensure effective response and support forensics

RS.MI.name: Incident Mitigation
RS.MI.description: Activities are performed to prevent expansion and mitigate effects of incidents

RC.RP.name: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
RC.RP.description: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of affected systems

A.5.23.name: Information security for use of cloud services

A.5.24.name: Information security incident management planning and preparation

A.5.25.name: Assessment and decision on information security events

A.6.3.name: Information security awareness, education and training
